tax_note: State-dependent tax rates; consider S-Corp election above $40K profit

vat_gst.name: Sales Tax
vat_gst.registration_threshold: Varies by state — economic nexus thresholds typically $100K in sales or 200 transactions
vat_gst.registration_threshold_note: No federal sales tax. Each state has its own rules. Many states exempt digital products entirely.
vat_gst.digital_products: Digital product taxability varies by state. Some states (e.g., Texas, New York) tax digital goods. Others (e.g., California, as of 2026) do not. Use TaxJar or Avalara to automate compliance.

digital_product_tax: No federal sales tax on digital products. State-level taxation is inconsistent — approximately 30 states tax digital goods in some form. Services like TaxJar, Avalara, or Stripe Tax handle multi-state compliance automatically for $50-500/month depending on volume.

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: All income taxed on personal return (Schedule C). Self-employment tax 15.3% on top of income tax.
entity_comparison.best_for: Testing a business idea; revenue under $20K/year

entity_comparison.setup_cost: $50-500 (state filing fee varies; Wyoming $100, California $70, Delaware $90)
entity_comparison.liability: Limited to company assets (if properly maintained)
entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Default: pass-through (same as sole prop). Can elect S-Corp taxation to reduce self-employment tax above ~$40K profit.
entity_comparison.best_for: Most indie developers; provides liability protection with pass-through taxation

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Pay yourself a 'reasonable salary' (subject to payroll tax). Remaining profit distributed as dividends (no self-employment tax). Saves money above ~$40K/year profit.

isp_note: Fiber available in major metros; Starlink for rural areas
disclaimer: This is general information only and does not constitute tax, legal, or financial advice. Tax law varies significantly by state. Consult a CPA or tax attorney licensed in your state for advice specific to your situation.
